programmes.show_description: Businessman Peter Williams chooses the founder of Apple, the abrasive but brilliant, Steve Jobs. With Matthew Parris. From 2017.

programmes.show_name: The Secret Diary of Adrian Mole, Aged 13 3/4
programmes.show_description: 4/10. Pandora organises a sock protest at school and Adrian finally gets his first passionate kiss. Read by Harry McEntire.
4/5. A pantomime provides a solo opportunity and a potentially bruised backside. Ronnie Corbett reads from his autobiography.
4/6. Martin Young's famous people quiz. With Francis Wheen, Fred Housego, Lynne Truss and Anthony Holden. From  2002.
6/6. Television is coming. For once, the Churches unite in moral outrage! The 1960s in the sleepy Irish town. Stars Gerard Murphy.
Sal and Tony get a surprise when visitors from abroad turn up - on the same day. Stars James Nickerson and Olwen May.
4 Extra Debut. Helen accompanies her son to an open day, dreading when he will leave the nest for university. Read by Claire Skinner.
4 Extra Debut. Mary broke up with Philip two years ago - so who is following her around the house now? Starring Maureen O'Brien.
5/5. Will there be a sickening end for our Slimmers' Club killer? What is the future for Alma and Jo? Stars Julie Hesmondhalgh.
3/6. Victoria's plans for a  perfect dinner party go awry. Stars Rosemary Leach, Celia Imrie and Nicola Pagett. From 1992.
6/8. Albert and Harold argue over buying a TV or a car. Adapted from BBC TV, with Wilfrid Brambell and Harry H Corbett. From 1972.
4/7. Mark Steel visits the city of Salisbury in Wiltshire and performs a bespoke stand-up show for a local audience. From 2022.
